title: conostylis crassinerva subsp. crassinerva
intro: Conostylis crassinerva subsp. crassinerva, also known as the stout-rhizome Conostylis, is a plant species within the family Haemodoraceae, native to Western Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Conostylis crassinerva subsp. crassinerva include the potential treatment of skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antifungal agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a decoction or infusion, where the leaves and roots are steeped in hot water to release their medicinal properties.
